input:
Your task is to predict the likely emotional responses of a character in this dialogue:

Harvey: You're late, Jess.
Jess: I know, Harvey. I'm sorry, I...
Harvey: Save it. I've heard it all before. You freelancers, always with the excuses.
Jess: It's not an excuse, Harvey. I had a project fall through, and...
Harvey: And what? You thought you'd just live here for free?
Jess: No, of course not. I just need a little more time.
Harvey: Time. Always time with you people. You know, when I was your age, I was running my own auto shop.
Jess: I know, Harvey. You've told me.
Harvey: And you think you've got it tough? Try losing everything to a crooked business partner.
Jess: I didn't say I had it tough, Harvey. I just...
Harvey: Just what? Thought you'd take advantage of an old man?
Jess: No! That's not it at all. I just...
Harvey: You just what? Spit it out, girl!
Jess: I just need a little more time, Harvey. That's all.
Harvey: And what if I don't have any more time to give, Jess?
[End dialogue]

At the end of this dialogue, Jess would feel...
Relieved
Fearful
Angry
Confused

Give each of these possible emotions a score from 0-10 for the relative intensity that they are likely to be feeling each.

You must output in the following format, including headings (of course, you should give your own scores), with no additional commentary:

Relieved: <score>
Fearful: <score>
Angry: <score>
Confused: <score>


[End of answer]
